title: Latent degradation-state features from a fold-safe state-space or Kalman filter
summary: Recent F1 work models tire wear as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simpler AR(1) benchmark.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tire wear as a latent stochastic process with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simpler AR(1) benchmark. That suggests the current handcrafted degradation proxies are still missing a useful hidden-state summary.
implementation_hint: Within each sorted `Race_Year x Driver` sequence, fit a lightweight online filter on lap-time residuals after removing coarse race effects. Export only past-informed states such as latent pace level, degradation slope, one-step forecast error, reset-adjusted pace, and posterior uncertainty; feed those into the existing tabular learner.
expected_effect: Improves detection of true ‘degradation tipping points’ versus noisy slow laps, especially when `TyreLife` and `Cumulative_Degradation` are ambiguous.
risk: If the filter is fit with future information or across train/test race segments, leakage will be severe. A too-heavy state model may also add variance without enough signal.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
